title: Choose PUBLIC, PRIVATE, or INTERFACE by who needs it
description: Mark a usage requirement PRIVATE when only the target itself needs it, INTERFACE when only consumers do, and PUBLIC when both do.
body: Use when adding includes, definitions, or link dependencies to a target. Do not mark everything PUBLIC to silence missing-symbol errors. Actions: ask whether a requirement is needed to build this target, to use it, or both; pick PRIVATE, INTERFACE, or PUBLIC accordingly; verify a consumer links cleanly without restating the dependency. Validation signals include a header-only dependency exposed as INTERFACE and an implementation detail kept PRIVATE. Risk boundary: over-broad PUBLIC visibility leaks implementation dependencies into the public interface and is hard to walk back once consumers rely on it.
